_note: REAL captured tool catalog of the official Atlassian remote MCP server (atlassian-rovo) as it advertised tools on 2026-07-15, replacing the prior SYNTHETIC 35-tool file (which was modeled on the community `sooperset/mcp-atlassian` server, a DIFFERENT server with full Jira CRUD). This is the actual surface an agent loads before its first action. IMPORTANT FINDINGS captured alongside this file (see .planning/phases/115-live-mcp-benchmark-re-measurement/115-MCP-SERVER-CHOICE.md): (1) the official atlassian-rovo server exposes only THESE 3 Teamwork Graph tools — there is NO editJiraIssue / createJiraIssue / updateJiraIssue / JQL-search tool, so it cannot perform the P115 benchmark task's 'edit 1 issue' step; (2) an actual tools/call with the ratified Bearer API-token is REJECTED with 'You don't have permission to connect via API token. Please ask your organization admin for access.' — the tools LOAD but do NOT function with this credential. No secrets are stored in this file.

_provenance.auth: Bearer API-token (Personal API Token; org-admin-gated) — value NOT stored here
_provenance.capture_method: Claude Code ToolSearch deferred-tool schema fetch from a session with cwd=reposix repo (atlassian-rovo local-scoped there); cross-checked against a nested `claude -p --output-format json` smoke session (session_id 52b94471-2c68-4d96-9dcf-7b4674b325a7) whose tool listing showed exactly these 3 mcp__atlassian-rovo__* tools
_provenance.invocation_error: You don't have permission to connect via API token. Please ask your organization admin for access.

server_instructions: Use getTeamworkGraphContext when retrieving or reasoning about relationships and connections between Atlassian entities - such as work items, people, teams, goals, projects, or third-party objects. Do not use Teamwork Graph tools for basic CRUD operations. After calling getTeamworkGraphContext, call getTeamworkGraphObject on key linked entities to retrieve richer detail.

tools.name: getTeamworkGraphContext
tools.description: Retrieves connected context from Teamwork Graph for any Atlassian entity. Returns all relationships and linked objects within one traversal – including cross-product and third-party connections. Use when the answer requires connections between entities, not just a single entity's fields. Supported entry points: Jira issues/projects/sprints/versions/comments; Confluence pages/blogposts/whiteboards/databases/spaces; Goals, Projects, Focus Areas, Tags and updates; People (users, teams, organisations); DevOps (PRs, repos, deployments, services, builds, designs); Loom videos/meetings; Compass components; Assets objects; Incidents, conversations, calendar events, external documents. After calling this tool, call getTeamworkGraphObject on returned ARIs to get full content.

objectType.description: Type of object to fetch context for.

objectIdentifier.description: Identifier for the object. It could be a key (e.g. ENG-1234), ID, ARI, or full URL; format depends on objectType.

after.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results.

first.description: Max number of related items to return.

relationshipTypes.description: Filter full results to specific relationship types to save tokens (e.g. 'jira-work-item-links-external-build' for Jira work items).

targetObjectTypes.description: Filter results to only include relationships targeting specific object types (e.g. AtlassianUser, ConfluenceComment).

timeRange.description: Optional time range to filter relationships by creation or update time.

from.description: ISO 8601 date-time string for the start of the time range (e.g. "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z").

to.description: ISO 8601 date-time string for the end of the time range (e.g. "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z").

tools.name: getTeamworkGraphObject
tools.description: Fetches the entire available data for one or more objects (Atlassian or third-party) using their ARIs or URLs. Use for the objects gathered from getTeamworkGraphContext tool.

objects.description: A list of object identifiers to hydrate. Supports both ARIs (e.g., 'ari:cloud:jira::issue/123') and full browser URLs (e.g., 'https://mysite.atlassian.net/browse/PROJ-1').

tools.name: addTeamworkGraphContext
tools.description: Adds a relationship between two entities in the Teamwork Graph (e.g. linking two Jira work items, marking one as blocking another, attaching a remote link, or connecting a Jira work item to an Atlas project or goal). NOTE: this is the ONLY mutating tool on this server, and it mutates the relationship graph only — it CANNOT edit an issue's own fields (summary, description, status).

objectIdentifier.description: Identifier of the source entity. Accepts an ARI, a full URL, or a stable key (e.g. ENG-123 for a Jira work item). Format depends on relationshipType.

relationshipType.description: Type of relationship to create. Determines which entity types 'objectIdentifier' and 'targetObjectIdentifier' must resolve to.

targetObjectIdentifier.description: Identifier of the target entity. Accepts an ARI, a full URL, or a stable key (e.g. ATLAS-20426 for an Atlas project). Format depends on relationshipType.

title.description: Optional display label for the target. Used by relationships that surface a title (e.g. remote links); ignored otherwise.
